chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI CP No.S-381 of 2023 (Mst. Fatima Zehra v. Muhammad Sheroz and others) ____________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) 1. For hearing of CMA No.3038/2023 2. For hearing of main case 26.11.2025 Ms. Faryal Ishaq, advocate for the petitioner Mr. Rasheed Ashraf, advocate for the respondent No.1 --------- The petitioner is aggrieved to the extent of modification of judgment dated 11.09.2021, whereby learned Civil and Family Judge, Malir, Karachi, in Family Suit No.05-A/2019 (re-Mst. Fatima Zahera and another v. Mr. Muhammad Sheroz) has fixed maintenance allowance at the rate of Rs.15000/- per month to the minor Baby Dua Sheroz with an increment of 15% per annum and through judgment dated 30.03.2023, learned appellate Court reduced the annual increment from 15th to 5% in Family Appeal No.63/2021 (re-Muhammad Sheroz v. Mst. Fatima Zahera and another). Learned counsel for the respondent has consented for the disposal of this petition that if increment is modified and fixed as 10% per annum. To such proposal, learned counsel for the petitioner has also expressed her agreement. By consent of the parties, this petition is disposed of. The judgment and decree dated 30.03.2023 passed by learned District Judge Malir Karachi and judgment and decree dated 11.09.2021 passed by Civil & Family Judge Malir, Karachi, are maintained and modified to the extent that the maintenance allowance of the minor Baby Dua Sheroz is fixed as Rs. 15000/- per month with an increment of 10% per annum from the date determined by the trial Court.

By consent of the parties, this petition is disposed of. The judgment and decree dated 30.03.2023 passed by learned District Judge Malir Karachi and judgment and decree dated 11.09.2021 passed by Civil & Family Judge Malir, Karachi, are maintained and modified to the extent that the maintenance allowance of the minor Baby Dua Sheroz is fixed as Rs. 15000/- per month with an increment of 10% per annum from the date determined by the trial Court. JUDGE Nadir/PS
